content.problemStatement:
Zawad is a rare competitive programmer — he takes his studies seriously. One day, while studying in a library, he found a pattern PPP in a century old book. The pattern is a string of lowercase English letters, with length MMM. Since Zawad is a curious boy, he started experimenting with the pattern.

In one experiment, Zawad considers all 26N26^N26N strings with length NNN that contain only lowercase English letters. The strings are numbered 000 through 26N−126^N - 126N−1 in lexicographical order. For each valid iii, let CiC_iCi​ be the length of the longest prefix of PPP which is also a suffix of the iii-th of these strings (0≤Ci≤M0 \le C_i \le M0≤Ci​≤M).

For each integer kkk between 000 and MMM inclusive, let SkS_kSk​ denote the sum of all valid indices iii such that Ci=kC_i = kCi​=k. Zawad wants to know all these sums, but since they could be huge, it is enough to compute them modulo 998,244,353998,244,353998,244,353.

Since Zawad is a bit busy at the library, he is asking for your help. Can you find the sums and earn Zawad's respect?
constraints: PPP contains only lowercase English letters
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first line of each test case contains two space-separated integers MMM and NNN.
The second line contains a single string PPP with length MMM.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print a single line containing M+1M+1M+1 space-separated integers S0,S1,…,SMS_0, S_1, \ldots, S_MS0​,S1​,…,SM​ — the sums modulo 998,244,353998,244,353998,244,353.
